tasks.title: Build ETNews AI/SW article crawler
tasks.description: Implement Python script to fetch and parse article listings from ETNews AI/SW section, extract article metadata (URL, title, date) and retrieve full article content
tasks.details:
Create `scripts/crawl.py` with:
- HTTP client using `requests` library (install via requirements.txt)
- BeautifulSoup4 for HTML parsing
- Fetch section listing from https://m.etnews.com/news/section.html?id1=04
- Parse article links matching pattern `https://etnews.com/YYYYMMDD000000`
- Extract article ID from URL (YYYYMMDD + 6-digit suffix)
- For each article link, fetch detail page and parse:
  - Title (from <h1> or article heading tag)
  - Published date (from meta tags or article date field)
  - Body text (from article content div/section, strip HTML tags)
  - Source URL
- Return list of Article objects: {id: str, url: str, title: str, published_at: str, raw_text: str, section: 'AI/SW'}
- Handle network errors gracefully with retry logic (exponential backoff, max 3 retries)
- Add user-agent header to avoid being blocked
- Log crawl progress and errors to stdout
- Implement as standalone function `crawl_etnews_section(section_url: str) -> List[Article]`
tasks.testStrategy: Manual testing: Run script locally and verify it fetches real articles from ETNews AI/SW section. Check article data structure contains all required fields. Test with actual ETNews URLs. Validate HTML parsing extracts title/body/date correctly. Test error handling by simulating network failures.

subtasks.title: Set up HTTP client with requests library and error handling
subtasks.description: Implement robust HTTP client setup with requests library, user-agent headers, timeout configuration, and exponential backoff retry logic for network failures
subtasks.details:
Create `scripts/crawl.py` foundation with:
- Import requests library and configure session with proper headers (user-agent to avoid blocking)
- Implement retry decorator/function with exponential backoff (max 3 retries, backoff factor 2)
- Set request timeout to 30 seconds
- Handle common HTTP errors (404, 500, connection errors, timeouts)
- Add logging for retry attempts and failures using Python's logging module
- Create reusable `fetch_url(url: str) -> requests.Response` function that handles all error cases
- Test with actual ETNews URLs to verify anti-blocking headers work
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test retry logic by mocking requests to simulate network failures. Test timeout handling. Verify exponential backoff timing. Test with real ETNews URLs to ensure headers prevent blocking. Check logging output contains retry attempts and errors.

subtasks.title: Parse section listing page with BeautifulSoup
subtasks.description: Implement BeautifulSoup HTML parsing to extract article links from ETNews AI/SW section listing page, handling Korean text encoding correctly
subtasks.details:
Add BeautifulSoup4 parsing for section listing:
- Fetch section listing from https://m.etnews.com/news/section.html?id1=04 using HTTP client from subtask 1
- Parse HTML response with BeautifulSoup (lxml parser for better Korean text handling)
- Identify article link container elements (inspect page to find correct CSS selectors/tags)
- Extract all <a> tags containing article links
- Filter links matching ETNews article URL pattern: `https://etnews.com/YYYYMMDD000000` using regex
- Handle encoding properly for Korean characters (ensure UTF-8)
- Return list of article URLs as strings
- Add logging for number of articles found
- Create function `parse_section_listing(html: str) -> List[str]`
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with saved HTML fixture of section page. Verify correct number of article links extracted. Test regex pattern matches valid URLs and rejects invalid ones. Check Korean text encoding is preserved. Test with actual live section page to verify selectors still work.

subtasks.title: Extract article IDs and validate URL patterns
subtasks.description: Implement article ID extraction from ETNews URLs using pattern matching, validate URL format, and create unique article identifiers
subtasks.details:
Build article ID extraction logic:
- Define regex pattern for ETNews article URLs: `https://etnews.com/(\d{8})(\d{6})`
- Extract date part (YYYYMMDD) and sequence number (6 digits) from URL
- Combine into article ID string: `YYYYMMDD` + sequence (e.g., '20240115123456')
- Validate URL format matches expected pattern, log warnings for unexpected formats
- Handle edge cases: malformed URLs, missing components
- Filter out non-article URLs (ads, category pages, etc.)
- Create function `extract_article_id(url: str) -> Optional[str]` returning ID or None if invalid
- Deduplicate article IDs to avoid fetching same article twice
- Log skipped/invalid URLs for debugging
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test with various URL formats (valid, invalid, edge cases). Verify correct ID extraction from valid URLs. Test deduplication works. Check invalid URLs return None. Test with real ETNews URLs from different dates.

subtasks.title: Fetch and parse article detail pages for metadata and content
subtasks.description: Implement article detail page fetching and HTML parsing to extract title, published date, and body text from individual ETNews article pages
subtasks.details:
Create article detail parser:
- For each article URL, fetch detail page using HTTP client from subtask 1
- Parse HTML with BeautifulSoup
- Extract title: find <h1> tag or article heading (inspect article pages for correct selector)
- Extract published date: search meta tags (property='article:published_time') or article date field, parse into ISO format string
- Extract body text: identify main article content container (likely <div class='article-body'> or similar), extract text content, strip HTML tags, preserve paragraph breaks
- Handle Korean text encoding properly (UTF-8)
- Remove ads, related articles, and other non-content elements
- Log warnings for missing fields
- Create function `parse_article_detail(html: str, url: str) -> dict` returning {title, published_at, raw_text}
- Handle parsing failures gracefully, return None for unparseable articles
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with saved HTML fixtures of real ETNews articles. Verify title, date, and body extracted correctly. Check Korean text preserved without encoding issues. Test with articles of different layouts. Verify HTML tags stripped from body. Test error handling with malformed HTML.

subtasks.title: Create Article data model and implement main crawler function
subtasks.description: Define Article dataclass/model structure and implement main crawler function that orchestrates fetching, parsing, and returns list of Article objects
subtasks.details:
Build main crawler interface:
- Define Article dataclass/TypedDict with fields: {id: str, url: str, title: str, published_at: str, raw_text: str, section: str}
- Implement `crawl_etnews_section(section_url: str) -> List[Article]` function that:
  - Fetches section listing page (subtask 2)
  - Extracts article URLs and IDs (subtask 3)
  - For each article URL, fetches and parses detail page (subtask 4)
  - Constructs Article object with all fields populated, section='AI/SW'
  - Filters out articles with missing required fields (title/date/body)
  - Returns list of valid Article objects
- Add progress logging: 'Processing article X of Y'
- Log summary: 'Successfully crawled X articles, Y failed'
- Add type hints throughout
- Make function idempotent and safe to re-run
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: Run crawler against real ETNews section URL. Verify returns list of Article objects with all required fields. Check article count matches expected. Validate article data quality (titles in Korean, dates in correct format, body text has content). Test end-to-end with logging output verification.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down the ETNews crawler implementation into: (1) HTTP client setup with requests library and error handling, (2) BeautifulSoup HTML parsing for section listing page, (3) Article link extraction and URL pattern matching, (4) Article detail page fetching and parsing (title/date/body), (5) Article data model and return structure. Each subtask should be testable independently.

tasks.title: Implement TF-IDF keyword extraction with KoNLPy
tasks.description: Create keyword extraction function using Korean morphological analysis (KoNLPy) and TF-IDF scoring to identify top N keywords from article text
tasks.details:
Create `scripts/extract.py` with keyword extraction:
- Install KoNLPy (requires JPype1 for JVM), konlpy, scikit-learn in requirements.txt
- Use KoNLPy's Okt (Open Korean Text) tokenizer for Korean text processing
- Tokenize article text into nouns/proper nouns (content words most meaningful for keywords)
- Filter out stopwords and single-character tokens
- Calculate TF-IDF scores using sklearn's TfidfVectorizer:
  - TF-IDF considers term frequency in document and inverse document frequency across all processed articles
  - For single-article processing, use simple term frequency with manual IDF-like weighting
- Extract top 5-10 keywords with highest scores
- Return keywords as list of strings ranked by score
- Function signature: `extract_keywords_tfidf(text: str, n_keywords: int = 7) -> List[str]`
- Handle Korean text encoding properly (UTF-8)
- Add error handling for JVM initialization failures (KoNLPy dependency)
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with sample Korean article text. Verify extracted keywords are relevant nouns/terms from the text. Test with articles of varying lengths. Manually inspect keyword quality for a few real ETNews articles. Verify it returns requested number of keywords.

subtasks.title: Set up KoNLPy and JPype1 dependencies in requirements.txt
subtasks.description: Add KoNLPy, JPype1, and scikit-learn to requirements.txt with pinned versions to enable Korean morphological analysis with JVM support
subtasks.details:
Add the following dependencies to requirements.txt:
- JPype1==1.4.1 (JVM bridge required by KoNLPy)
- konlpy==0.6.0 (Korean NLP library)
- scikit-learn==1.3.2 (for TfidfVectorizer)

JPype1 provides the Java Virtual Machine bridge that KoNLPy requires for its underlying Korean analyzers. KoNLPy will be used specifically with the Okt (Open Korean Text) tokenizer. Include installation notes in comments about JDK requirements (Java 1.7+ needed). Consider platform-specific installation issues (Windows may need additional setup).
subtasks.testStrategy: Create fresh virtual environment and run `pip install -r requirements.txt`. Verify all three packages install without errors. Test `import konlpy` and `from konlpy.tag import Okt` to confirm JVM initialization works. Document any platform-specific installation issues encountered.

subtasks.title: Implement Korean text tokenization with Okt tokenizer
subtasks.description: Create tokenization function using KoNLPy's Okt analyzer to extract nouns and proper nouns from Korean article text
subtasks.details:
In `scripts/extract.py`, create helper function `tokenize_korean(text: str) -> List[str]`:
- Initialize Okt tokenizer: `okt = Okt()`
- Use `okt.nouns(text)` to extract all nouns and proper nouns (most meaningful content words for keywords)
- Handle UTF-8 encoding properly for Korean text
- Add try-except block for JVM initialization errors with informative error messages
- Return list of extracted noun tokens
- Consider caching Okt instance to avoid repeated JVM initialization overhead
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test with sample Korean text containing various nouns, proper nouns, and other parts of speech. Verify only nouns/proper nouns are extracted. Test with UTF-8 encoded strings. Test error handling by simulating JVM initialization failure. Verify extracted tokens are valid Korean morphemes.

subtasks.title: Implement stopword filtering and token cleanup
subtasks.description: Create filtering logic to remove Korean stopwords, single-character tokens, and low-quality terms from tokenized text
subtasks.details:
In `scripts/extract.py`, create `filter_tokens(tokens: List[str]) -> List[str]`:
- Define Korean stopword list (common words like: 것, 등, 때, 수, 년, 월, 일, etc.)
- Filter out single-character tokens (usually not meaningful as keywords)
- Remove tokens that are purely numeric or special characters
- Optionally filter extremely common domain terms that appear in every article
- Consider minimum token length threshold (e.g., >= 2 characters)
- Return cleaned token list
- Keep filtering rules configurable for future tuning
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with token list containing stopwords, single chars, numbers, and valid keywords. Verify stopwords are removed. Verify single-character tokens filtered out. Test edge cases like empty input, all-stopword input. Manually review filtered results for a sample article to ensure meaningful terms retained.

subtasks.title: Implement TF-IDF scoring with scikit-learn
subtasks.description: Create TF-IDF calculation logic using sklearn's TfidfVectorizer to score term importance, adapted for single-document context
subtasks.details:
In `scripts/extract.py`, create `calculate_tfidf_scores(tokens: List[str]) -> Dict[str, float]`:
- For single-document processing, use term frequency with manual weighting since traditional IDF requires corpus
- Use sklearn's TfidfVectorizer with parameters:
  - analyzer='word'
  - token_pattern for Korean (or use preprocessed tokens)
  - max_features=None (we'll select top-N later)
- Alternative: Use simple term frequency (Counter) with length normalization if TfidfVectorizer doesn't fit single-doc scenario well
- Return dictionary mapping each unique token to its TF-IDF score
- Normalize scores to [0, 1] range for interpretability
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with sample tokenized Korean article. Verify TF-IDF scores are calculated correctly (higher for distinctive terms, lower for common ones). Test that scores are normalized properly. Compare results with manual TF calculation to verify correctness. Test edge case with single unique token.

subtasks.title: Implement top-N keyword selection logic
subtasks.description: Create function to select and rank top N keywords based on TF-IDF scores with configurable keyword count
subtasks.details:
In `scripts/extract.py`, implement `select_top_keywords(score_dict: Dict[str, float], n: int) -> List[str]`:
- Sort tokens by TF-IDF score in descending order
- Select top N tokens (default N=7 based on requirements)
- Return as list of strings ranked by score (highest first)
- Handle edge case where fewer than N unique tokens available
- Consider adding minimum score threshold to filter out low-quality keywords
- Optionally return tuples of (keyword, score) for debugging/logging purposes
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with score dictionary of varying sizes. Verify correct number of keywords returned. Verify keywords are sorted by score descending. Test edge cases: n > available keywords, n=0, empty score dict. Manually review top keywords from real article to assess quality and relevance.

subtasks.title: Create main extract_keywords_tfidf function with error handling
subtasks.description: Build the primary public API function that orchestrates tokenization, filtering, scoring, and selection with comprehensive error handling
subtasks.details:
In `scripts/extract.py`, implement `extract_keywords_tfidf(text: str, n_keywords: int = 7) -> List[str]`:
- Function flow: tokenize_korean() → filter_tokens() → calculate_tfidf_scores() → select_top_keywords()
- Add comprehensive error handling:
  - Try-except for JVM initialization failures (KoNLPy dependency) with informative error message
  - Handle UTF-8 encoding errors gracefully
  - Validate input (non-empty text, positive n_keywords)
  - Return empty list or raise clear exception on failure
- Add logging for debugging (token count, filtering stats, top keywords)
- Include docstring with usage examples
- Consider caching Okt instance at module level to avoid repeated JVM initialization
subtasks.testStrategy: End-to-end test with real Korean ETNews article text. Verify complete pipeline returns 7 relevant keywords by default. Test with varying n_keywords values (3, 5, 10). Test error cases: empty text, invalid n_keywords, simulated JVM failure. Manually review extracted keywords from 3-5 real articles for quality and relevance.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down Korean keyword extraction into: (1) KoNLPy and dependencies installation/setup (JPype1 for JVM), (2) Korean text tokenization with Okt tokenizer, (3) Stopword filtering and token cleanup, (4) TF-IDF scoring implementation with scikit-learn, (5) Top-N keyword selection logic, (6) Error handling for JVM initialization and encoding issues. Focus on Korean-specific challenges.

tasks.title: Implement TextRank extractive summarization
tasks.description: Create summarization function using TextRank algorithm to generate 2-5 sentence extractive summary from article text
tasks.details:
Add to `scripts/extract.py`:
- Install sumy library for TextRank summarization (requires nltk)
- Use sumy's TextRankSummarizer with Korean text support
- Configure to extract 3-5 most important sentences from article
- TextRank algorithm:
  - Builds graph of sentences with edges weighted by sentence similarity
  - Applies PageRank to identify most central/important sentences
  - Returns top-ranked sentences in original document order
- Preprocessing: split article into sentences (use Korean-aware sentence tokenizer)
- Post-processing: join selected sentences with proper spacing
- Function signature: `summarize_textrank(text: str, sentence_count: int = 4) -> str`
- Handle edge cases: very short articles (< sentence_count), single-paragraph articles
- Preserve original sentence order in output summary
- Add fallback: if TextRank fails, return first N sentences as summary
tasks.testStrategy: Test with sample Korean articles of different lengths. Verify summary length matches requested sentence count. Check that summary sentences appear in original document order. Manually review summary quality for coherence and informativeness. Test edge cases (very short/long articles).

subtasks.details: Implement `preprocess_sentences(text: str) -> List[str]` function in extract.py. Use nltk's Korean punkt tokenizer for sentence boundary detection. Handle Korean-specific punctuation (。, !, ?, etc.). Clean whitespace and normalize sentence formatting. Test with various Korean article structures (multiple paragraphs, quotes, lists). Return list of sentence strings ready for TextRank processing.

subtasks.title: Configure and execute TextRank algorithm with sumy
subtasks.description: Implement core TextRank summarization using sumy's TextRankSummarizer to extract most important sentences
subtasks.details: Implement `summarize_textrank(text: str, sentence_count: int = 4) -> str` in extract.py. Initialize sumy's TextRankSummarizer with Korean language support. Use PlaintextParser from sumy to parse preprocessed text. Configure sentence_count parameter (default 4, range 2-5). Execute TextRank algorithm to rank sentences by importance. Extract top N sentences based on PageRank scores. Handle the case where requested sentence_count exceeds available sentences in article.

subtasks.title: Implement post-processing, sentence ordering, and fallback handling
subtasks.description: Add post-processing to preserve original sentence order in summary and implement fallback strategies for edge cases
subtasks.details: Extend summarize_textrank function with post-processing: (1) Preserve original document order of selected sentences by sorting by their position in source text. (2) Join sentences with proper Korean spacing. (3) Implement fallback: if TextRank fails (exception, empty result), return first N sentences as summary. (4) Handle edge cases: very short articles (<sentence_count), single-paragraph articles, empty/invalid input. Add try-except blocks around TextRank execution. Return fallback summary using simple sentence extraction when needed.

tasks.title: Build Jekyll Markdown post generator
tasks.description: Create post generator that converts article metadata, keywords, and summary into Jekyll-compatible Markdown files with proper YAML front matter and filename conventions
tasks.details:
Create `scripts/generate_post.py`:
- Function signature: `generate_post(article: Article, keywords: List[str], summary: str, output_dir: str = '_posts') -> str`
- Generate filename: `_posts/YYYY-MM-DD-<slugified-title>.md`
  - Extract date from article.published_at
  - Slugify title: lowercase, remove special chars, replace spaces with hyphens, max 50 chars
  - Use python-slugify library for proper Korean→ASCII slug conversion
- YAML front matter template:
  ```yaml
  ---
  layout: post
  title: "[Original Article Title]"
  date: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS +0900
  categories: [etnews, ai-sw]
  tags: [keyword1, keyword2, ...]
  source_url: "https://etnews.com/YYYYMMDD000000"
  auto_generated: true
  ---
  ```
- Markdown body template:
  ```markdown
  ## Keywords
  keyword1, keyword2, keyword3, ...

  ## Summary
  [Summary text here]

  ---
  *This post was automatically generated from [ETNews AI/SW section](source_url). [Read original article →](source_url)*
  ```
- Create `_posts/` directory if it doesn't exist
- Write file with UTF-8 encoding
- Return generated file path for logging
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test with mock Article, keywords, summary data. Verify YAML front matter is valid (test with Jekyll parser or YAML library). Check filename follows YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md convention. Verify file content renders correctly by running `bundle exec jekyll serve` locally and viewing generated post. Test Korean character handling in title/slug/content.

tasks.title: Implement article deduplication and state tracking
tasks.description: Create state management system to track processed article IDs and prevent duplicate post generation across multiple runs
tasks.details:
Create `scripts/state.py` and `data/processed_articles.json`:
- State file structure: `{"processed_ids": ["20250719000001", ...], "last_run_at": "2025-07-19T09:00:00+09:00"}`
- Implement StateManager class:
  - `load_state(state_file: str = 'data/processed_articles.json') -> dict`
  - `save_state(state: dict, state_file: str) -> None`
  - `is_processed(article_id: str, state: dict) -> bool`
  - `mark_processed(article_id: str, state: dict) -> dict`
- State file location: `data/processed_articles.json` (committed to repo)
- Initialize state file if doesn't exist: `{"processed_ids": [], "last_run_at": null}`
- Filter articles in crawler: skip any article whose ID is in processed_ids list
- After successful post generation, add article ID to processed_ids
- Update last_run_at timestamp after each run
- Handle state file corruption gracefully: if JSON parse fails, backup corrupt file and create fresh state
- Ensure state file is committed after each run (included in GitHub Actions commit)
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: test state file creation, loading, saving, dedup logic. Integration test: run crawler+generator twice, verify second run produces no duplicate posts. Test state file corruption recovery. Manually verify state file is valid JSON and contains expected article IDs.

tasks.title: Create orchestrator script to run end-to-end pipeline
tasks.description: Build main entry point script that coordinates crawler, extractor, generator, and state management in correct sequence with error handling and logging
tasks.details:
Create `scripts/run_daily.py` as main entry point:
- Flow: Load state → Crawl articles → Filter processed → Extract (keywords+summary) → Generate posts → Update state → Commit state file
- Pseudo-code:
  ```python
  def main():
      # Setup logging to stdout
      logger = setup_logging()
      
      # Load state
      state = load_state()
      
      # Crawl
      articles = crawl_etnews_section('https://m.etnews.com/news/section.html?id1=04')
      logger.info(f'Crawled {len(articles)} articles')
      
      # Filter processed
      new_articles = [a for a in articles if not is_processed(a.id, state)]
      logger.info(f'{len(new_articles)} new articles to process')
      
      if not new_articles:
          logger.info('No new articles, exiting')
          return
      
      # Process each article
      for article in new_articles:
          try:
              keywords = extract_keywords_tfidf(article.raw_text)
              summary = summarize_textrank(article.raw_text)
              post_path = generate_post(article, keywords, summary)
              mark_processed(article.id, state)
              logger.info(f'Generated post: {post_path}')
          except Exception as e:
              logger.error(f'Failed to process article {article.id}: {e}')
              continue  # Skip failed article, process others
      
      # Save state
      save_state(state)
      logger.info('Pipeline completed')
  ```
- Add CLI argument: `--dry-run` to test without writing files/state
- Error handling: catch exceptions per article to avoid failing entire batch
- Log summary statistics: articles crawled, processed, skipped, failed
- Exit code 0 on success, 1 on critical failure (no articles crawled, state corruption)
tasks.testStrategy: End-to-end integration test: Run script locally with real ETNews data, verify complete flow works. Test dry-run mode. Test error scenarios (network failure, invalid article data). tasks.title: Build GitHub Actions workflow for daily automation
tasks.description: Create GitHub Actions workflow that runs daily on schedule, executes the pipeline script, and commits generated posts automatically
tasks.details:
Create `.github/workflows/daily-post.yml`:
```yaml
name: Daily ETNews Digest

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 1 * * *'  # Run daily at 1 AM UTC (10 AM KST)
  workflow_dispatch:  # Allow manual trigger

jobs:
  generate-post:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write  # Required to push commits
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Install Java (for KoNLPy)
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: 'temurin'
          java-version: '11'
      
      - name: Install Python dependencies
        run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run daily pipeline
        run: python scripts/run_daily.py
      
      - name: Commit and push if changes
        run: |
          git config --local user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git config --local user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git add _posts/ data/processed_articles.json
          git diff --staged --quiet || git commit -m "chore: auto-generated daily digest [skip ci]"
          git push
```
- Cron schedule: 1 AM UTC = 10 AM KST (Korean business hours)
- workflow_dispatch enables manual testing via GitHub UI
- Commit message includes `[skip ci]` to prevent recursive workflow triggers
- Use actions/cache for pip to speed up dependency installation
- Only commit if there are actual changes (git diff --staged --quiet)
- Error handling: workflow will fail visibly if pipeline script exits with error code

Test scheduled trigger by temporarily setting cron to run in a few minutes. Confirm GitHub Pages rebuilds after workflow push.

subtasks.title: Implement config loading and validation logic
subtasks.description: Create config loader module with validation, error handling, and default fallback values
subtasks.details:
Create `scripts/config.py` module:

```python
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, Any

DEFAULT_CONFIG = {
    'crawler': {'section_url': 'https://m.etnews.com/news/section.html?id1=04', 'lookback_days': 7, 'request_timeout': 30, 'max_retries': 3},
    'extraction': {'keyword_method': 'tfidf', 'keyword_count': 7, 'summarization_method': 'textrank', 'summary_sentences': 4},
    'post': {'output_mode': 'per_article', 'categories': ['etnews', 'ai-sw'], 'layout': 'post'},
    'state': {'file_path': 'data/processed_articles.json'}
}

def load_config(config_path: str = 'config.yml') -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Load and validate configuration file with fallback to defaults."""
    if not Path(config_path).exists():
        print(f"Warning: {config_path} not found, using defaults")
        return DEFAULT_CONFIG
    
    with open(config_path) as f:
        config = yaml.safe_load(f)
    
    # Validate required sections
    required_sections = ['crawler', 'extraction', 'post', 'state']
    for section in required_sections:
        if section not in config:
            raise ValueError(f"Missing required config section: {section}")
    
    # Validate enum values
    valid_keyword_methods = ['tfidf', 'keybert', 'llm']
    if config['extraction']['keyword_method'] not in valid_keyword_methods:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid keyword_method: {config['extraction']['keyword_method']}")
    
    valid_summarization_methods = ['textrank', 'llm']
    if config['extraction']['summarization_method'] not in valid_summarization_methods:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid summarization_method: {config['extraction']['summarization_method']}")
    
    return config
```

Include validation for all enum fields and required keys.

```python
from config import load_config

def main():
    # Load configuration
    config = load_config('config.yml')
    logger.info(f"Loaded config: {config}")
    
    # Load state with config path
    state = load_state(config['state']['file_path'])
    
    # Crawl with config parameters
    articles = crawl_etnews_section(
        url=config['crawler']['section_url'],
        lookback_days=config['crawler']['lookback_days'],
        timeout=config['crawler']['request_timeout'],
        max_retries=config['crawler']['max_retries']
    )
    
    # Extract with config parameters
    for article in new_articles:
        keywords = extract_keywords_tfidf(
            article['text'],
            n_keywords=config['extraction']['keyword_count']
        )
        summary = summarize_textrank(
            article['text'],
            sentence_count=config['extraction']['summary_sentences']
        )
    
    # Generate posts with config
    generate_posts(
        articles,
        categories=config['post']['categories'],
        layout=config['post']['layout']
    )
```

subtasks.details:
Add **GitHub Actions** section to README.md:

**Automated Daily Execution**:
- Workflow runs automatically every day at 1 AM UTC (10 AM KST)
- Schedule defined in `.github/workflows/daily-update.yml` cron expression: `0 1 * * *`
- Workflow steps: Checkout → Setup Python → Install dependencies → Run pipeline → Commit changes → Push to repo

**Manual Trigger**:
- Navigate to Actions tab in GitHub repository
- Select "Daily ETNews Update" workflow
- Click "Run workflow" button (workflow_dispatch trigger)
- Useful for testing or running pipeline on-demand

**Viewing Logs**:
- Go to Actions tab → Click workflow run → View job logs
- Check "Run pipeline" step for crawl/extract/generate output
- Verify "Commit changes" step shows new posts committed

**Troubleshooting Common Failures**:
- **Network/crawl errors**: ETNews site may be down or changed HTML structure → Check crawler selectors in scripts/crawl.py
- **KoNLPy/JVM errors**: Java not installed in GitHub Actions runner → Verify workflow installs Java (e.g., `actions/setup-java@v3`)
- **State file conflicts**: Concurrent runs or manual edits → Delete `seen_articles.json` and re-run (will reprocess recent articles)
- **No new articles**: ETNews section had no new posts in lookback period → Normal, workflow succeeds with no changes
- **Jekyll build errors**: Invalid front matter in generated posts → Check post_template in config.yml
